context:
Rule: ARGB Voltage 5V Strict Requirement
Category: compatibility
Type: validation
Components: motherboard + rgb
Expression: [object Object]
output:
❌ INCOMPATIBLE:

Issue: ARGB LEDs require exactly 5V. Connecting to 12V RGB header will instantly destroy all LEDs.

Solution: Verify header voltage before connecting. 5V ARGB = 3-pin. 12V RGB = 4-pin. Use correct header.

Category: compatibility
Type: validation
Components: motherboard + rgb
Expression: [object Object]
output:
⚠️ WARNING:

Warning: ARGB chains over 300 LEDs (~5 meters) may experience signal degradation causing flickering or wrong colors.

Solution: Limit ARGB chain to 200-250 LEDs. Use separate header or powered splitter for additional strips.

metadata.rule_name: ARGB Chain Length Limit Signal Degradation

instruction: Check compatibility between motherboard and rgb
context:
Rule: ARGB Splitter Powered vs Passive
Category: compatibility
Type: validation
Components: motherboard + rgb
Expression: [object Object]
output:
⚠️ WARNING:

Warning: Passive ARGB splitters limited to ~100 LEDs total. Powered splitters support 200+ LEDs per port.

Solution: Use powered ARGB splitter (SATA/molex power) for 3+ strips or 150+ total LEDs. Prevents dimming/flickering.

output:

Solution: Purchase Corsair Commander Core XT or Lighting Node Pro to control Corsair RGB devices. Required for iCUE ecosystem.

instruction: Evaluate compatibility: motherboard + rgb
context:
Rule: RGB Software Conflicts Multiple Brands
Category: compatibility
Type: validation
Components: motherboard + rgb
Expression: [object Object]
output:
⚠️ WARNING:

Warning: Running multiple RGB software (Aura + iCUE + Mystic Light) causes conflicts and high CPU usage.

Solution: Use only one RGB ecosystem when possible. Or use universal software (OpenRGB, SignalRGB) to control all devices.

instruction: Check compatibility between motherboard and rgb
context:
Rule: RGB Daisy Chain All Devices Single Header
Category: compatibility
Type: validation
Components: motherboard + rgb
Expression: [object Object]
output:
⚠️ WARNING:

Warning: Daisy-chaining all RGB devices to single header limits to 3A (36W). Exceeding causes flickering or failure.

Solution: Use multiple ARGB headers or powered splitter for 4+ devices. Check motherboard header amp rating.

metadata.rule_name: RGB Daisy Chain All Devices Single Header

instruction: Analyze GPU and Case compatibility
context:
Rule: gpu_length_rtx_4090
Category: physical
Type: requires
Components: GPU + Case
Expression: [object Object]
output:
❌ INCOMPATIBLE:

Issue: RTX 4090 (336-355mm) requires case with 340mm+ GPU clearance

Solution: Use full-tower or mid-tower case with 350mm+ GPU support

instruction: Determine if GPU and Case are compatible
context:
Rule: gpu_length_340mm_full_tower_v2
Category: physical
Type: requires
Components: GPU + Case
Expression: [object Object]
output:
❌ INCOMPATIBLE:

Issue: 340mm+ GPU (ASUS TUF RTX 4090) requires full-tower or XL mid-tower with 350mm+ clearance

Solution: Use Fractal Torrent (461mm), Lian Li O11 XL (420mm), Corsair 7000D (420mm), or similar full-tower

instruction: Analyze GPU and Case compatibility
context:
Rule: physical_rtx4090_case_length
Category: physical
Type: requires
Components: GPU + Case
Expression: [object Object]
output:
❌ INCOMPATIBLE:

Issue: RTX 4090 is 300-360mm long - requires case with 360mm+ GPU clearance

Solution: Use full tower or large mid-tower (Fractal Torrent, Lian Li O11D)

instruction: Evaluate compatibility: Case + Motherboard
context:
Rule: case_mini_itx_itx_board
Category: physical
Type: requires
Components: Case + Motherboard
Expression: [object Object]
output:

Issue: Mini-ITX cases only support Mini-ITX motherboards

Solution: Use Mini-ITX motherboard for compact build

instruction: Verify if Motherboard works with Case
context:
Rule: ATX motherboard requires ATX+ case
Category: physical
Type: form_factor
Components: Motherboard + Case
Expression: [object Object]
output:
❌ INCOMPATIBLE:

Issue: ATX motherboards require ATX mid-tower or larger case

Solution: Cannot fit in Micro-ATX or Mini-ITX cases

instruction: Verify if case works with cooler
context:
Rule: CPU Cooler Height Mini-ITX Strict Limits
Category: physical
Type: validation
Components: case + cooler
Expression: [object Object]
output:
❌ INCOMPATIBLE:

Issue: CPU cooler exceeds Mini-ITX case height limit. ITX cases have strict limits (70-160mm).

Solution: Check case specifications for exact CPU cooler height limit. Consider low-profile coolers (under 70mm) or AIO liquid cooling for ITX builds.
